originalTitle: Ex-teacher accused of Scotland school abuse found guilty of sex attack in South Africa
title: Making Things Right: A Fair Decision in South Africa!
content:
Hello, Super Kids of Africa!

Hello, super kids of Africa! We have some important news to share today from a very special place right here on our amazing continent: South Africa! It’s a big, beautiful country with lots of sunshine, incredible animals, and wonderful people. Sometimes, grown-ups make choices that are not good, and it's important that everyone knows what happens next when that happens. We are here to talk about a very serious story, but also a story about fairness and safety.

What is a Court?

Imagine a special building where grown-ups go to talk about rules and fairness. This place is called a court! In a court, there's a very important person called a judge. The judge listens to everyone very, very carefully. They listen to stories, sometimes called 'evidence,' and look at clues to help them decide what is fair and right. It’s a bit like when you play a game, and there’s a referee to make sure everyone follows the rules. This week, something important happened in a court in a city called Cape Town, which is in South Africa.

A Story from Long, Long Ago

This story is about a grown-up named Iain Wares. A very, very long time ago, Mr. Wares used to be a teacher. A person who was a student many, many years ago, when they were much younger, said that Mr. Wares did something that made them feel unsafe and unhappy. This happened in the 1980s. That was even before many of your parents were born! It shows that even if something happened a long time ago, it can still be important to talk about it.

The Court's Decision: Finding Out What's Right

The judge in the Cape Town court listened to everything with great care. They listened to the story from the person who was a student, and they also listened to Mr. Wares. After listening to all the information and thinking hard, the judge made a very important decision. The judge decided that Mr. Wares did something wrong. The court said he was 'guilty' of not treating his student well. This means the court found enough proof that he broke important rules about how to treat children.

What Does 'Guilty' Mean?

When a court says someone is 'guilty,' it means they have found proof that the person did something wrong. It means the rules were broken, and the person is responsible for their actions. It’s like when you accidentally break a window playing football – you are responsible for breaking it. In this case, the court made a very serious decision. This decision helps make sure that everyone is safe and treated fairly. It also shows that no one is above the law, no matter how much time has passed. Did you know? Africa has 54 beautiful countries! South Africa is one of the biggest and most famous ones. It's known for its stunning wildlife like lions and elephants, and its amazing people!

Why It's Always Important to Speak Up

This story reminds us of something super important for every child: it’s always, always okay to tell a trusted grown-up if someone makes you feel unsafe, sad, scared, or confused. A trusted grown-up could be your mom, dad, grandparent, auntie, uncle, or even another teacher or community leader you feel safe with. They are there to help you! Even if something happened a long time ago, like in this story, it's never too late to tell the truth. Telling the truth is a very powerful thing.

Schools Must Be Safe Places

Schools are special and important places where you go to learn new things, play games, and make wonderful friends. They should always, always feel safe. Grown-ups who work at schools, like teachers, principals, and assistants, are there to help you learn and, most importantly, to keep you safe. Their job is to make sure every child feels happy, respected, and protected while they are at school. If you ever feel unsafe at school, remember to speak up to a trusted adult. Did you know? Your school has rules to keep you safe! Things like not running in the hallways, walking with a grown-up when you leave the classroom, and being kind to your friends are all important safety rules!

Justice and Fairness for Everyone

When the court makes a decision like this, it’s called 'justice.' Justice means that things are made fair, and people who do wrong things are held responsible for their actions. It helps everyone feel safer knowing that there are rules and that grown-ups will listen if something is not right. It shows that telling the truth is powerful, and that even after many years, bad actions can be addressed. This helps make sure that bad things don't go unnoticed and that people are held accountable.

Looking Out for Each Other

We all have a part to play in keeping our communities safe and happy. If you ever see or hear something that makes you worry about a friend, or if you feel worried about yourself, remember to tell a grown-up right away. Even small worries are important to share, because grown-ups can help make things better. We all look out for each other, just like a big, loving family. Did you know? In many African cultures, children are seen as a special gift, and the whole community helps to raise and protect them! It's like having many aunties and uncles looking out for you all the time!

A Message to All Kids

Remember, you are important, and your feelings matter very much. You have a right to be safe, happy, and loved. Always remember that there are grown-ups who care about you and want to protect you. Don't be afraid to use your voice if you ever feel unsafe. Your voice is strong, and it can make a difference in making the world a safer place for everyone. Be brave, be kind, and stay safe, super kids of Africa!
summary: In South Africa, a court found a former teacher, Iain Wares, guilty of harming a student many years ago, showing that telling the truth and seeking justice can make things right, even after a long time.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows that grown-ups in charge, like judges, work to make sure everyone is safe and treated fairly. It teaches us that it's super important to tell a trusted grown-up if someone makes you feel unsafe or sad, because your voice can help bring justice and keep everyone protected.
